description: Single source of truth for techtrip-secondbrain. bin/precheck.sh audits the machine against this; every bin/setup-*.sh consults it. macOS MVP.

binaries.label: flock
binaries.note: Runtime dependency of claude-obsidian's wiki-lock.sh + allocate-address.sh (per-file write locking, DragonScale address counter). Not shipped with macOS; both call sites fail hard without it.

binaries.label: Python 3
binaries.note: Runtime dependency of claude-obsidian's setup-retrieve.sh and setup-mode.sh. Usually present via Xcode CLT; declared so precheck/doctor verify it on fresh machines.

binaries.label: yt-dlp
binaries.note: Only needed by the yt-fetch skill. Not installed by setup-deps; bin/setup-features.sh installs it (the youtube feature is the default-yes freebie during setup).

binaries.label: WhisperKit
binaries.note: Only needed by the voice-fetch skill (on-device audio transcription, CoreML/Neural Engine). Not installed by setup-deps; bin/setup-features.sh installs it. First transcription downloads a CoreML model once (network + disk); transcription itself is fully local.

binariesNote: optional:true binaries are NOT installed by setup-deps.sh (they power an optional skill/feature); precheck reports them as 'optional' not 'missing', and bin/setup-features.sh installs them on demand.

claudePlugins.author: AgriciDaniel
claudePlugins.note: Original plugin by AgriciDaniel (https://github.com/AgriciDaniel/claude-obsidian), MIT. Installed from TechTripAi's patched fork (TechTripAi/claude-obsidian) because upstream is backlogged — the fork carries fixes plus maintenance additions filed upstream as candidate PRs (e.g. the invalid SessionStart prompt hook, upstream issue #116; the 1.9.3 maintenance suite), and tracks upstream via git remote for periodic sync. Attribution and license remain AgriciDaniel/MIT. The CLI can't pin versions, so testedVersion is advisory: precheck, setup-claude-obsidian, and doctor surface a non-failing drift note when the installed version differs.

obsidianPluginsNote: Each plugin is PINNED to a release tag, and every downloaded asset is verified against its sha256 before install (supply-chain guard: a hijacked upstream release fails loudly instead of installing silently). Refresh pins + hashes with: bash scripts/pin-obsidian-plugins.sh

obsidianPlugins.note: Required for the Obsidian MCP server. Key generated locally in setup-mcp.sh.
~8MB main.js; pulled from release so the vault is self-contained.

mcpServers.name: obsidian
mcpServers.note: OBSIDIAN_API_KEY is injected by setup-mcp.sh to match the Local REST API plugin key. NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0 accepts the REST plugin's self-signed cert, but it disables TLS verification for EVERY outbound TLS connection this MCP server process makes, not just the localhost one — acceptable only because mcp-obsidian talks solely to 127.0.0.1:27124. Do not reuse this env for any server that reaches the network; the cleaner per-CA alternative would be exporting the plugin cert and pointing NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS at it.

harnesses.note: bin/setup-harnesses.sh makes the vault harness-agnostic: symlinks installed plugin skills into the cross-vendor skill dirs (Cursor/Codex discovery) and stamps AGENTS.md + Cursor hook/rule parity artifacts into the vault (never overwrites existing files). Re-run after bin/update.sh so links re-point at the new plugin version.

skills.note: Setup orchestrator: drives the bin/ steps end-to-end (precheck → … → doctor).
Integrity check + MCP repair; wraps bin/doctor.sh and bin/repair-mcp.sh.
Instructional usage tutorial. No runtime deps; teaches by handing the user prompts to run — executes nothing and never touches the vault.

Greenfield origination-project scaffolder: stamps wiki/projects/<slug>/ from the origination template (node-based, no extra deps). Vault-side templates + origination-workflow.md are seeded by setup-vault.sh from assets/vault/; the script self-seeds the templates on pre-existing vaults.

optionalFeaturesNote: bin/setup-features.sh enables any of these on demand (idempotent, re-runnable); the secondbrain skill asks about each one inline during setup and drives this script per answer. defaultEnabled:true = harmless freebie, prompt defaults to YES (still confirm-gated, still skippable). consentNote = why the feature needs an explicit opt-in; setup-features prints it before asking, prompt defaults to NO. NotebookLM has no dedicated binary — uv is already required by the MCP server — so enabling it means the notebooklm-py CLI install + one-time interactive `notebooklm login`. Syncthing support was removed (git is the only sync path); setup-sync.sh offers to remove a legacy vault .stignore but never uninstalls Syncthing itself — external software is the user's to manage.

optionalFeatures.label: YouTube transcripts (yt-fetch)
optionalFeatures.desc: Fetch a YouTube video's transcript + metadata into .raw/videos/ for ingestion. Low-risk freebie: a passive CLI binary — no daemon, no network listener, no credentials — so setup enables it by default (Enter accepts; 'n' skips).

optionalFeatures.label: Voice memos / audio transcription (voice-fetch)
optionalFeatures.desc: Transcribe local audio (Voice Memos .m4a, mp3, wav, …) into .raw/audio/ for ingestion. Fully on-device (WhisperKit, CoreML/Neural Engine) — no cloud, no credentials, no daemon — so setup enables it by default. Heads-up worth saying at the prompt: the first transcription downloads a CoreML model once (can be GBs; local-only after that).

optionalFeatures.label: NotebookLM synthesis (notebooklm-ingest)
optionalFeatures.consentNote: Sends the sources you feed it to Google's NotebookLM for synthesis (data leaves your machine), and needs a one-time interactive Google OAuth login — so it stays off until you explicitly say yes.
optionalFeatures.desc: Offload multi-source synthesis to Google NotebookLM, then ingest the report. Installs the unofficial notebooklm-py CLI via uv, then needs a one-time interactive `notebooklm login`.
